Daniela, what do you think? Should we adopt the new platform this quarter?
From my perspective, it is a good tool, but it might be a little fast for this quarter.
I think we are ready. Waiting is the bigger risk.
I see your point, but I am not sure the team is trained yet. The downside is a rushed launch.
That is a valid concern. So what do you suggest?
It seems to me a small pilot first would be reasonable. Perhaps two stores.
A pilot. I like that. You almost persuaded me.
Then I agree: we pilot in two stores, and we do not overstate the risk.
In my view, we should test it first.
It might take a little longer than planned.
I see your point, but I am not sure that is the best option.
That is a valid point, and it sounds reasonable.
I do not want to overstate the downside.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about giving opinions at work. In a professional meeting, how you say your opinion matters as much as the opinion itself. Notice three things. First, I open with a clear phrase. In my view, the new tool will save us time. Second, when I am not completely sure, I soften the idea. It seems to me that the rollout might take a little longer than planned. Words like might, perhaps, and a little are softeners. They make me sound careful, not weak. Third, when I disagree, I stay polite. I see your point, but I think we should test it first. I never say you are wrong. Try this in your next meeting. State your view, soften it when needed, and disagree with respect. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. Thanks for sharing your opinion in the meeting today. I want to give you some honest feedback, the kind a colleague gives. Overall, you did well. You opened with a strong phrase. From my perspective, we should adopt the new platform. That was clear and confident. One small thing. When the team disagreed, you said they were wrong. I would soften that a little. Try something like, I see your point, but I am not sure that is the best option. It sounds more open, and people listen more. Also, when you are not certain, it is fine to hedge. Say, it might work, or perhaps we could try a pilot first. That is not weak. It is professional. Keep stating your views. Just add a little softness, and you will sound like a senior manager. Talk soon.
